etymology.description: 舌(舌) (shé, "tongue") + 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet") = tongue tasting sweetness -> sweet
etymology.interpretation: The character 甜 represents the concept of sweetness through the combination of 舌(舌) (shé, "tongue") and 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet"). The tongue is the organ responsible for tasting, especially sweetness, which is captured in this character. This intuitive link between tasting and sweetness forms the core meaning of the character 甜.

examples.chinese: 这块蛋糕非常甜(這塊蛋糕非常甜)。
examples.english: This piece of cake is very sweet.

examples.chinese: 他的笑容很甜(他的笑容很甜)，让人感到温暖(讓人感到溫暖)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiàoróng hěn tián, ràng rén gǎndào wēnnuǎn.
examples.english: His smile is sweet and makes people feel warm.

examples.chinese: 夏天的西瓜特别甜(夏天的西瓜特別甜)。
